[LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) No courtesy copy of the petition was received by the assigned examiner. A cursory review of it online shows that there is no prayer in the petition. (4) It appears that this personal representative was required to be bonded for $305,000, not $350,000. (5) It is unclear why bank statements are attached when no accounting is presented. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/10/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORES HEARRING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent's codicil required the creation of a testamentary trust for the benefit of Winston Hearring. The decedent designated the executor and Delores Anderson as co-trustees of the testamentary trust to hold and manage Winston Hearring's 2/5 share of the estate funds to purchase his home. The executor sold the estate's most valuable asset, a 1/5 interest in real property in San Francisco, for $160,000 and distributed the proceeds in equal shares to the 4 devisees (including the executor) without prior court approval of preliminary distribution. Winston Hearring agreed to receive 1/4 of the proceeds, rather than 2/5 given by the codicil, in exchange for immediate outright distribution to purchase his home in Texas. The estate funds on hand in the total amount of $5,486 will be exhausted for partial payment of statutory fees of the executor's former and/or current attorneys. There will be no estate funds left to create and fund the testamentary trust for Winston Hearring. Probate Code section 15202 provides that "a trust is created only if there is trust property". 2) The executor's former attorney has not waived partial statutory fees for services rendered to the executor before the former attorney was discharged in 2021. Section 10814 and CRC 7.704 require the statutory fees to be apportioned between both attorneys according to the services actually rendered by each attorney or as agreed to by the attorneys. No agreement has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD CORNELIUS COUGHLIN III
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/11/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of the notice of this hearing has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The creditor, whose claim will not be paid, is entitled to notice. (No Allowance or Rejection of the claim appears to have been filed, as required by Probate Code Section 9250(b).) (4) The "accounting" for the business raises some issues. The source of most of the deposits is not shown. Disbursements show many "transfers" to Linda, a wire transfer of $19,5000 to Derick J. Oluoch, another $20,000 wire transfer without the name of the recipient, as well as payments for a Toyota, many other transportation/travel costs (gas, Fastrak, and Lyft), dental costs and personal expenses (e.g., $13,898.41 + $1,250.00 several times to Carmax, $2,747.69 to Best Buy, $1,026.00 for landscaping, meals, dues and subscriptions, air travel, 23 and Me), and $24,205.02 "Payments to Others" with a question mark. These do not appear to be related to the business. (5) Was the business closed or relocated? Paragraph 20 of the petition is unclear. If the business has been closed, then, there is nothing to distribute if that was the only asset of the estate. Deadline is 5/31/24 for providing the assigned examiner a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. (The Court will not make orders that were not part of the petition prayer.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Although the administrator and her attorney are not actually requesting fees and costs, if they were, the administrator's time sheets would need to be filed and costs would need to be itemized. The petition would also need to be amended to clearly show in the caption that the administrator is seeking extraordinary compensation. [CRC 7.102.]) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTIS BYRD JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/16/2024 to be calendared with the related cases (PES-18-301880, PES-20-304064, and PES-21-304896) involving the same real property co-owned by each estate. It should be noted that the decedent's surviving spouse, Jeanette Byrd, did not file a timely election under Probate Code section 13502(a)(1) to probate her intestate share (one-half) of the decedent's separate real property interest otherwise passing to her without administration. See sections 13500 & 13501. It is unclear whether the surviving spouse intends to file a spousal property petition or apply for court approval to make a late election under section 13502(b), or whether the personal representative intends to petition under section 13502.5 to compel a fractional interest passing to the surviving spouse to be administered with a showing of good cause required by that section.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/11/24. (No earlier availability.) 1) A copy of the ex parte petition filed on 2/2/24 and the proposed order still have not been left with the clerk in Room 103, as required by LRSF 14.11 B., in order for the clerk to log it into the tracking spreadsheet so that it can be assigned to an examiner. 2) No notice of the ex parte petition has been filed either. [LRSF 14.14 C.] 3) The ex parte petition does not fully meet the requirement of CRC 7.52. [CRC 7.52: "Petitioner must file a declaration describing efforts made to locate a person entitled to notice in a proceeding under the Probate Code, but whose address is unknown, before the court will prescribe an alternate form of notice or dispense with notice under (c). The declaration must state the name of the person whose address is unknown, the last known address of the person, the approximate date when the person was last known to reside there, the efforts made to locate the person, and any facts that explain why the person's address cannot be obtained. The declaration must include a description of the attempts to learn of the person's business and residence addresses by: (1) Inquiry of the relatives, friends, acquaintances, and employers of the person entitled to notice and of the person who is the subject of the proceeding; (2) Review of appropriate city telephone directories and directory assistance; and (3) Search of the real and personal property indexes in the recorder's and assessor's offices for the county where the person was last known or believed to reside."] Deadline is 5/31/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILTON DEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues have not been addressed since this petition was initially set for 11/01/2023 and since counsel's appearance at the last hearing on 02/21/2024: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). A review of the amended petition and other papers the petitioner has filed since the last hearing on 02/21/2024 indicates that the following issues must be addressed: 1) Re the DE-121 form filed on 04/04/2024: i) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) was not attached. ii) Item 1 must be completed. iii) The word "AMENDED" (petition) must be inserted where applicable. 2) Re the AMENDED petition filed on 04/04/2024: i) Item 5a(2) was not checked. ii) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. iii) The decedent died more than 4 years ago. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. 3) Re the proof of publication filed on 04/25/2024: The published notice did not refer to the "AMENDED" petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
